purpose: Audit whether the seven Phase 2 catalog conflicts contain a directly recoverable title-artist relation before using them as mechanistic examples.

prompt_templates.template:
Who performs the song titled "{title}"? Reply with only the artist name.
Artist:

scoring.generated_match_normalization: Unicode NFKC, casefold, alphanumeric characters only
scoring.primary_margin: median across templates of target mean token logprob minus emitted mean token logprob

conflict_categories.reference_recoverable: reference generated in at least 2 of 3 prompts and primary margin > 0
conflict_categories.generation_only: reference generated in at least 2 of 3 prompts and primary margin <= 0
conflict_categories.margin_only: reference generated in fewer than 2 prompts and primary margin > 0
conflict_categories.persistent_emitted_binding: emitted artist generated in at least 2 prompts, reference generated in fewer than 2 prompts, and primary margin <= 0

claim_boundaries: This is an exploratory audit of seven previously observed conflicts, not a confirmatory test.
Reference recovery is evidence of behavioral accessibility, not subjective awareness.
Failure to recover does not prove that the model never encoded the relation.
The catalog-supported reference is not asserted to be the only real-world artist for a title.
No hidden-state, circuit, intervention, prevalence, or self-correction claim is permitted.
This audit cannot reopen the stopped Phase 2 H1 or H2 analyses.
downstream_rule: Only reference_recoverable conflicts may be treated as candidate known-relation errors in the next mechanistic discovery stage; all other conflicts remain catalog mismatches without a latent-knowledge claim.
